name: Litigation & Advocacy
student_summary: Argue cases in court — the classic advocate's path.
overview: Argue cases in court — the classic advocate's path. Litigation & Advocacy sits in the Legal Practice family of Law, Government & Public Policy. It is a broad career direction — you can enter it through several routes and specialize later without restarting.
who_is_this_for: You keep asking why until you reach the bottom of things
who_should_avoid: You want a short, easy study path — this one demands years of serious preparation
Intense competition drains you — entry here is a contest
You want a laptop-anywhere lifestyle — this work happens in person
daily_responsibilities: Apply legal research to real problems
Collaborate with your team using drafting
Keep improving at oral argument — the field keeps moving
Communicate progress, results and decisions to the people who depend on them
work_environment: Laboratories and research facilities with focused desk analysis
alternative_paths: Company Secretary route to corporate governance
scope: Demand outlook in India is booming; see FAQs for how the platform keeps this current.
advantages: Strong earning ceiling (₹60 LPA+ at senior levels)
A broad Legal Practice foundation that opens multiple specializations later
challenges: Fierce competition for the best seats and roles
A long, demanding preparation and learning curve
Location-bound work; postings and relocation are part of the deal
Early years demand patience before the rewards compound
misconceptions: “You must pick your exact job title now.” Litigation & Advocacy is a direction — specialization comes later.
“Only toppers make it.” Consistent skill-building beats one exam rank in the long run.
portfolio_ideas: A documented case study of drafting
projects: Beginner: explore legal research with a small self-chosen project
Intermediate: build or run something real using Legal databases
smaller_city_scope: Viable in smaller cities — demand exists well beyond the metros.
rural_scope: Limited rural presence; expect to base yourself in a town or city.
home_state_note: Ask the AI coach about your state — regional demand, cities and relocation advice are generated live from retrieved data, never hardcoded.
transition_paths: Toward Judiciary & Judicial Services: build the shared skills and take a bridging project or course
Toward Corporate Law: build the shared skills and take a bridging project or course
Toward Criminal Law: build the shared skills and take a bridging project or course
faqs: What does Litigation & Advocacy pay in India?
Entry roles typically start around ₹3 LPA and senior professionals reach ₹60 LPA or more. Live, region-specific salary data is retrieved by the platform when you ask.
What should I study after Class 12?
Focus on English, Polity. Typical degrees: 5-year BA LLB (via CLAT) or 3-year LLB, Bar Council enrolment. Alternatives exist: 3-year llb after any degree.
Will AI take over this career?
AI impact is minimal — deeply human/physical work. Broad paths like Litigation & Advocacy evolve rather than disappear — the students who pair domain skill with AI tools benefit most.
Is this a broad path or a specific job?
Litigation & Advocacy is a career direction. Once inside it you can specialize further — the platform will map specializations in future versions.
